ks1.objective: Listen for your name and run to your side before your partner catches you.
successCriteria: Stays cheerful whether tagged or escaped

lks2.objective: Develop reaction time and acceleration — beat your partner to your side or catch them before they reach theirs.
successCriteria: First step is sharp and decisive
Doesn't false-start on the wrong call
Adjusts strategy as they read the coach

uks2.objective: Combine quick reactions, acceleration and deception — read the coach's call early without committing too soon.
successCriteria: Stays balanced and ready in the middle
Accelerates explosively on the correct call
Uses footwork to evade a tag at close range

equipment.item: Cones to mark the two sides

space.description: Two opposing sides marked with cones. Children pair up and stand side-by-side in the middle of the playing area.

steps: Children work in pairs, standing side-by-side in the middle of the area.
One child is 'Santa', the other is 'Elves'.
If 'Santa!' is called — Santas run to the Santa side. Elves chase and try to tag them before they arrive.
If 'Elves!' is called — Elves run to the Elves side. Santas chase.
Reset in the middle and play another round.
questions: How did you use your speed to reach your side before being tagged?
How did you stay confident and keep trying even if you got tagged?

step.space: Make the distance to the side bigger or smaller
step.task: Pass a ball to each other — when the teacher calls, the person holding the ball escapes
step.equipment: Tuck a bib in as a 'tail' to pull instead of tagging
